channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Mali drone strikes kill at least 10 civilians at wedding
item.description: Mali's military carries out strikes after attacks last month by al-Qaeda-linked fighters and Tuareg separatists.

item.title: Iran war day 80: Trump issues warning; Tehran ready to confront aggression
item.description: The fear of renewed US strikes in Iran looms while Israeli attacks continue in Lebanon despite extended 'ceasefire'.

item.title: Earthquake hits southern China, toppling buildings
item.description: A magnitude 5.2 earthquake has struck southern China, causing over a dozen buildings to collapse, killing at least two.

item.title: Britain faces weeks of leadership limbo in slow-motion coup against Starmer
item.description: Keir Starmer battles to retain his authority, critics say he's a 'lame-duck' leader.

item.title: Growing bread queues in Gaza as Israel restricts fuel, flour imports
item.description: Bread shortages deepen in Gaza as flour imports dwindle and bakeries struggle to meet rising demand amid fuel scarcity.

item.title: Sinner wins Italian Open to complete career Golden Masters
item.description: The first Italian to win the title in 50 years enters the French Open as favourite with Alcaraz out injured.

item.title: Former UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting announces bid to replace Starmer
item.description: Former UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting has announced he will run against Prime Minister Keir Starmer as Labour leader

item.title: When are Hajj and Eid al-Adha 2026? A visual guide to the Muslim pilgrimage
item.description: Nearly 2 million Muslims will embark on the annual Hajj pilgrimage to Mecca, which begins on May 25.

item.title: Uganda responds with emergency measures to Ebola outbreak
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Catherine Soi reports on the emergency measures implemented in Uganda in response to the Ebola outbreak.

item.title: US says China to buy billions in agricultural goods after Trump-Xi talks
item.description: China will buy 'at least' $17bn worth of US agricultural goods annually, the White House says.

item.title: Moment of collision between two Navy jets at Idaho air show
item.description: Two US Navy jets collided during an air show at Mountain Home AFB.

item.title: Israel launches strikes on southern Lebanon despite extending ‘ceasefire’
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Obaida Hitto reports from Tyre in southern Lebanon on the latest Israeli strikes in the region.

item.title: How ISWAP and Boko Haram are reshaping the Lake Chad Basin
item.description: Communities in the Lake Chad Basin face rising violence amid economic hardship and weak governance.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran says talks ongoing through Pakistan
item.description: Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson says talks are ongoing through Pakistan as the mediators.

item.title: Pakistan interior minister holds talks with Iranian president in Tehran
item.description: Pakistan Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi met Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian in Tehran

item.title: Trump holds prayer rally to ‘rededicate’ US as ‘one nation under God’
item.description: Critics have denounced the event as a blurring of the separation of church and state mandated by the US Constitution.

item.title: ‘Won’t be anything left’: Trump issues threat to Iran amid stalled talks
item.description: The US president has pressured Iran to concede to demands including dismantling nuclear programme and missile stocks.

item.title: Israel kills at least five in Lebanon after ‘ceasefire’ extended
item.description: At least five people have been killed in Israeli air attacks on several locations in southern and eastern Lebanon.

item.title: DRC struggling to contain Ebola outbreak as cases spread
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo has faced repeated Ebola outbreaks.

item.title: Will the latest Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda spread further?
item.description: The World Health Organization declares the epidemic a global health emergency.

item.title: Mexican officials announce 10 dead in early-morning shooting in Tehuitzingo
item.description: Officials blamed the shooting on 'armed individuals', though no details were provided about attackers or their motives.

item.title: Israel kills three Palestinians in strike on an aid kitchen in Gaza
item.description: Israel bombed a kitchen that provided meals to the displaced in Gaza, killing three and injuring others.

item.title: Peruvian authorities confirm Fujimori and Sanchez advance to runoff
item.description: Candidates will face off on June 7 election after first round of voting marred by logistical errors, fraud allegations.

item.title: France listening to 10 ‘new’ suspected victims of Epstein: Prosecutor
item.description: French magistrates investigate possible offences involving French perpetrators who facilitated Jeffrey Epstein's crimes.

item.title: Sudanese army advances in Blue Nile state deepen displacement crisis
item.description: The Sudanese army has made advances in Blue Nile state, with renewed fighting forcing families into overcrowded camps
